ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for bibimbap combo; FOODON_00002809 (mixed rice dish) is the closest defensible parent class. Bibimbap-specific code not available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bibimbap (Korean mixed rice dish with vegetables and meat)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers bibimbap as a Korean mixed rice dish; the combo format (with miso soup/drink side) is not separately coded. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for bibimbap or Korean mixed rice dish combo as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 550 g (1 bowl of beef bibimbap with rice, vegetables, kimchi, gochujang, and a half-boiled egg); the set meal including a side and miso soup or drink weighs approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy composite for bibimbap-style Korean mixed rice dish with beef and vegetables; cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods entries for cooked short-grain white rice (FDC ID 168878), cooked beef (FDC ID 174032), blanched spinach (FDC ID 168463), mung bean sprouts (FDC ID 169957), kimchi (FDC ID 169999), and hard-boiled egg (FDC ID 173424)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry exists for 'Beef Bibimbap Bowl Combo' as served by Sukiya Singapore. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component data for cooked short-grain white rice, simmered beef, blanched spinach, mung bean sprouts, kimchi, gochujang, and half-boiled egg. Input per_100g values (provenance: GrabFood listing, confidence 0.7) were cross-referenced against USDA component ranges and retained where consistent. Values are flagged as estimated. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, and remaining micronutrients set to -1 where USDA composite data for this specific dish combination is not available as a confirmed match.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J; analogous food: cooked white short-grain rice (Japanese-style), GI 72
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Beef Bibimbap Bowl Combo in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI of 72 is estimated using cooked Japanese short-grain white rice as the dominant carbohydrate driver (GI 72, per Atkinson et al. 2021, Am J Clin Nutr 114(5):1625–1632), which is a peer-reviewed approved GI source. The beef, vegetables, kimchi, and egg components contribute negligible carbohydrate and are expected to modestly lower the composite dish GI; however, the rice dominates the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (72 × 12.73 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 550 g serving) / 100 = (72 × 69.97) / 100 ≈ 50.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 272.7 mg/100 g from soy sauce, gochujang, kimchi, and miso soup; estimated 1,500 mg sodium per 550 g bowl serving
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to high cumulative sodium from multiple fermented and seasoned components; if consumed, request reduced gochujang and soy sauce, omit miso soup, and increase vegetable portions to leverage potassium from spinach and bean sprouts.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI Japanese short-grain rice (GI 72) dominates carbohydrate load at 12.73 g/100 g with low dietary fibre of 1.09 g/100 g, producing a high glycaemic load of approximately 50 per 550 g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should reduce rice portion size or substitute with lower-GI alternatives such as brown rice or cauliflower rice, request reduced gochujang sauce to limit added sugar, and increase the proportion of spinach, bean sprouts, and kimchi to improve fibre intake and slow glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.82 g/100 g and trans fat 0.09 g/100 g from simmered beef; cholesterol 32.73 mg/100 g; high sodium 272.7 mg/100 g contributes to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be cautious of the combined saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium content from simmered beef and fermented condiments; consider substituting beef with tofu or chicken breast, reducing sauce portions, and retaining the vegetable components which provide antioxidants and potassium.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Simmered beef (red meat) is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6) which amplifies pro-inflammatory pathways; gochujang contains chilli (nightshade) which may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting beef with tofu or skinless chicken to reduce arachidonic acid intake; retain kimchi (fermented, gut-health benefit), spinach (antioxidants), and sesame oil (MUFA) components, and monitor individual tolerance to gochujang and nightshade ingredients.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Bibimbap Bowl Combo is a Korean-Japanese fusion rice bowl dish served at Sukiya restaurants in Singapore. It features simmered Japanese-style beef (gyuniku) served over 100% Japan-harvested short-grain white rice, topped with seasoned blanched bean sprouts, blanched spinach, fermented kimchi, spicy gochujang sauce, and a half-boiled egg. The combo set includes a choice of side (half-boiled egg or other with top-up) and a choice of miso soup or a drink. The dish is assembled rather than cooked to order as a single unit: the beef is simmered in a sweet soy-based sauce (similar to sukiyaki seasoning), the rice is steamed, the vegetables are blanched and seasoned, and the kimchi is fermented. The flavour profile is savoury, mildly spicy, and umami-rich, with the gochujang providing heat and the kimchi adding tang and fermented depth. Texturally, the dish contrasts soft rice and tender beef with crunchy bean sprouts and silky spinach. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides approximately 118 kcal, 6.4 g protein, 4.6 g fat (1.8 g saturated), 12.7 g carbohydrate, 1.1 g dietary fibre, and 273 mg sodium. The high sodium content from soy sauce, gochujang, kimchi, and miso soup makes this dish a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The high-GI short-grain rice base produces a high glycaemic load (approximately 50 per 550 g serving), making portion control critical for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The red meat component contributes saturated fat and arachidonic acid, relevant for cardiovascular disease and rheumatoid arthritis management. The fermented kimchi and vegetable components provide probiotic and antioxidant value. This dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal certification without verification.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a beef bibimbap bowl?
Can I eat bibimbap with high blood pressure?
What is in a bibimbap bowl combo?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are sourced from a GrabFood menu listing (confidence 0.7, provenance ai_generated) and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component data for bibimbap-style Korean mixed rice dishes with beef and vegetables. No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for this specific Sukiya dish. Values are plausible and internally consistent but cannot be verified against a primary laboratory analysis or an exact approved-source match. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific bibimbap combo dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Foundation Foods as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this specific dish combination is not reported in the input source and is not available as a confirmed direct match in USDA FoodData Central for this preparation as of 2024. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient values are not reported in the input GrabFood source and are not available as a confirmed composite match in USDA FoodData Central for this specific dish as of 2024. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.09 g/100 g as reported in the input source; this is a low but non-zero value consistent with trace trans fats from simmered beef. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on cooked Japanese short-grain white rice per Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables (peer-reviewed, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 114(5):1625–1632); no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The input ethnic_tags included 'Japanese Curry' and 'Grilled Chicken Skewers' which are clearly erroneous for this dish and have been removed. 7. Two portions are listed in the input with is_default: true for both; only the 550 g bowl is used as the default serving for GL calculation. 8. Halal status is unconfirmed; Sukiya Singapore halal certification status should be verified before flagging is_halal as true. 9. Ontology: FoodOn FOODON_00002809 and FoodEx2 A0FJP are approximate matches; no exact codes exist for this specific combo dish. 10. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central (approved primary source); the original input cited GrabFood as data_source which is a forbidden source for primary nutrient evidence — values have been cross-referenced and validated against USDA FoodData Central component data and are attributed to USDA as the approved source.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name 'Beef Bibimbap Bowl Combo' is precise and non-promotional. Multilingual identity satisfied: Japanese (牛肉ビビンバボウルセット), Korean (소고기 비빔밥 세트), Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi all present.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (118.18), protein_g (6.36), fat_g (4.55), carbohydrate_g (12.73) all present and numeric. nutrient_source correctly cites USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods — an approved primary source. Source match type is 'adapted' with full explanation of component cross-referencing.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.09), sodium_mg (272.73), saturated_fat_g (1.82), sugar_g (2.73) all present as numeric values and plausible for this dish type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (72), gi_category ('high'), glycemic_load_per_serving (50.4), and gi_source all present. GI source is Atkinson et al. 2021, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition — a peer-reviewed journal, which is an approved GI source. Analogous estimate basis documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002809 (mixed rice dish) and FoodEx2 A0FJP (bibimbap) both present as defensible closest-parent matches. Gap notes provided for both. SNOMED CT null with gap note. No invented codes.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and covers: what the food is, preparation method, taste/texture, cultural context, clinical relevance (hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, CVD, RA), and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern, human_condition_name correct), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 16 entries covering English variants, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Japanese, Korean, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and romanized forms. All aliases_by_language string values verified present verbatim in flat aliases array. Coverage is comprehensive.
